summary: Data-intensive SaaS for IT financial planning and cost transparency at enterprise scale.
highlights: Marwan led a migration to solve a scaling and cost problem: Kubernetes and Java pipelines were running for days and getting expensive as data volume grew.
He migrated targeted heavy workloads to EMR Spark with Scala for throughput on large datasets.

He built a parallel reconciliation pipeline so legacy and new systems ran side by side on the same inputs and flagged discrepancies in real time — enabling incremental cutover with zero correctness drift on financial datasets.
The migration delivered roughly 10x faster processing while maintaining service availability.
He reduced manual operations using Python, AWS Lambda, Step Functions, and Terraform-managed AWS infrastructure.

summary: Cloud directory and device management at organizational scale.
highlights: Marwan directed end-to-end technical design and delivery of a GraphQL-based Microsoft M365 integration serving more than 50,000 tenants.

He designed a multi-tenant GraphQL schema and production resolvers, the API layer, and a hybrid PostgreSQL plus MongoDB architecture: the core identity graph in Postgres for fast relational queries, per-tenant custom attributes isolated in MongoDB for variability and high write volume.
He delivered the integration end to end using Docker, Golang, gRPC, JavaScript, and Node.js.

summary: Subscription commerce and service delivery for consumer printing at global scale.
highlights: Marwan planned and executed scaling HP's Instant Ink subscription service from thousands of customers to millions, globally.

He transformed a Ruby on Rails monolith into cloud-native microservices and event-driven data pipelines on AWS using Domain-Driven Design and Test-Driven Development — Lambda, API Gateway, SNS, SQS, DynamoDB, Redshift, CloudFormation — coordinating engineering, DevOps, QA, and product across four geographically distributed teams.
The program completed in a matter of months with service reliability and stability maintained throughout.

summary: TQL — SQL-like ticketing query language for operations
highlights: Built front-end and back-end components on a service-oriented single-page application for ticket and task management.
Designed RESTful APIs, async JavaScript modules, and custom query languages for unstructured operational data.
Mentored new developers and helped raise code-quality standards across the team.
